businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla is transitioning from EV manufacturing to an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform. FY2025 revenue $94.8B (-2.9% YoY), net income $3.79B (-46.5% YoY). Friday close $391.20 (-0.96%). Terafab AI chip fab confirmed for March 21 (Saturday) launch. Optimus 3 robot production starting this summer. Tesla-xAI joint 'Macrohard (Digital Optimus)' project and xAI-to-SpaceX equity conversion accelerating Musk ecosystem integration.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)
keyProducts: Terafab - In-house AI chip fab (3/21 launch)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extremely high valuation reflecting AI, robotics, and energy platform premium

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects FY2026 improvement expectations but still among highest in sector

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 decline driven by delivery drops and price cuts

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Margin compression from pricing competition. Well below 2022 levels

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: Solid cash generation. Total cash of $44B

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Excellent financial health. Among lowest in large-cap tech

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Below large-cap tech peers. Margin recovery is key

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Neutral to bearish zone. About 7% below 50-day moving average

growthDrivers.title: Terafab AI Chip Fab Confirmed for 3/21 (Saturday) Launch
growthDrivers.description: Reuters (3/14): Musk confirmed Terafab project launch on 3/21. Tom's Hardware, FinTechWeekly confirmed the date. AI5 chip in-house production facility, ending NVIDIA dependency. Low-volume production in 2026, mass production by 2027.

growthDrivers.title: Optimus 3 Robot Production Starting This Summer
growthDrivers.description: Teslarati (3/14): Musk confirmed Optimus 3 low-rate production starting this summer. S-curve manufacturing ramp-up. New designs annually. Mass production target by 2027.

growthDrivers.title: China Sales +35% YoY in Jan-Feb, 127,728 Units
growthDrivers.description: Finbold (3/15): Made-in-China Tesla EV sales surged 35%+ YoY in the first two months, reaching 127,728 units. BYD numbers declined in the same period. European Feb registrations also +10% YoY.

growthDrivers.title: UK Electricity Supply License, Energy Business European Expansion
growthDrivers.description: Reuters (3/12): UK regulator Ofgem approved Tesla Energy Ventures electricity supply license. Direct power sales to UK homes and businesses. Expanding the Texas model to Europe.

growthDrivers.title: Tesla-xAI Joint 'Macrohard (Digital Optimus)' Project
growthDrivers.description: Reuters (3/11): Musk unveiled joint Tesla-xAI project 'Macrohard' targeting software market disruption. xAI investment converted to SpaceX equity ahead of SpaceX IPO.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader. Vertical integration across software, manufacturing, energy, and FSD's 8.2B miles of data. Terafab adds AI chip self-sufficiency. xAI and SpaceX connections expand the Musk ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelmingly dominant training data vs competitors.
Own batteries (4680), custom chips (HW4 to AI5 Terafab), software (FSD), charging (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader. Strong on pricing. Dominates Chinese domestic market

recentNews.headline: Finbold: Tesla breaks below 200-day moving average for first time since September. 20%+ decline from ATH enters bear market territory
recentNews.significance: Closed below the 200-day MA ($395-400 zone). Over 20% decline from December ATH meets traditional bear market definition. Sustained institutional selling pressure

recentNews.headline: Terafab AI chip fab launch confirmed for March 21 (Saturday). Tom's Hardware, FinTechWeekly verify
recentNews.significance: AI5 in-house chip production facility. Ending NVIDIA dependency. Mass production by 2027. Launch success is the week's biggest catalyst

recentNews.headline: European Feb registrations +10% YoY, 17,425 units sold. First meaningful growth in a year
recentNews.significance: 10% increase across 15 major European markets. However, YTD roughly flat vs last year, so not time to celebrate yet

recentNews.headline: Tesla wins UK electricity supply license. Can sell power directly to homes and businesses
recentNews.significance: Ofgem approval opens UK power market entry. Expanding the Texas model to Europe. New energy revenue stream

recentNews.headline: Institutional selling accelerates: Calydon Capital -62.3%, Bamco -8.2% position reduction
recentNews.significance: Multiple institutions trimming positions in Q3 13F filings. Post-ATH profit-taking and valuation concerns

analystOpinions.notableComment: MarketBeat consensus $406.84 (+4.0%). Majority Hold among 27 analysts. Bulls go as high as $600, but the 200-day MA breakdown calls for caution.

risks.title: 200-Day Moving Average Breakdown, Bear Market Entry
risks.description: Finbold (3/15): First close below 200-day MA since September. Over 20% decline from ATH meets traditional bear market definition. Further downside pressure possible.

risks.title: Institutional Selling Accelerating
risks.description: Calydon Capital -62.3%, Bamco -8.2%. Multiple institutions trimmed Q3 positions. Institutional selling adding downward pressure.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation at P/E 362x
risks.description: TTM P/E 362x, Forward P/E 189x. Any growth slowdown could trigger sharp multiple compression.

risks.title: Potential Third Consecutive Year of Delivery Decline
risks.description: Reuters: Investors and analysts cutting FY2026 delivery estimates. Revenue and earnings further downside risk.

risks.title: Musk Leadership Distraction
risks.description: Simultaneously running Tesla, xAI, SpaceX, X (Twitter), and DOGE. Persistent CEO focus concerns.

buyReasons.title: Terafab AI Chip Launch 3/21, Hardware Self-Sufficiency
buyReasons.rationale: AI5 in-house production ends NVIDIA dependency. AI hardware cost reduction + supply security. Launch success would be a near-term bounce catalyst.

buyReasons.title: China Sales +35% YoY, 127,728 Units Beating BYD
buyReasons.rationale: BYD declined while Tesla gained share. Combined with European Feb +10%, global sales recovery signal is emerging.

buyReasons.title: Energy Business European Expansion, UK Power Supply
buyReasons.rationale: Ofgem license enables direct power sales to UK consumers. Megapack + power supply diversifies energy revenue.

buyReasons.title: Optimus 3 Commercialization, Robotics Era Begins
buyReasons.rationale: Production starting this summer, mass production by 2027. Success opens $12T+ robotics TAM.

overallOpinion: Terafab AI chip factory launch is confirmed for this Saturday (3/21). If successful, Tesla will produce AI chips in-house without NVIDIA. China sales also surged 35% in Jan-Feb to 127,728 units, outpacing BYD.

On the bearish side, the stock broke below the 200-day moving average for the first time since September, and the 20%+ decline from ATH officially enters bear market territory. Institutional selling is accelerating (Calydon -62%, Bamco -8%).
Trading at $391 with P/E 362x, below both the 50-day ($420) and 200-day ($425) averages. Target $407 (+4%) offers limited upside. Wait for Terafab launch results before making a move.

investmentVerdict.summary: Terafab 3/21 launch and China sales surge are positive, but the 200-day MA breakdown and institutional selling create headwinds. P/E 362x with only 4% upside to target. Wait for Terafab results.

sources.name: Finbold
sources.description: Tesla 200-day MA breakdown, bear market analysis

sources.name: IBTimes
sources.description: Terafab launch + xAI + SpaceX IPO analysis

threads.hook: Tesla broke 200-day MA, Saturday Terafab is the wildcard
threads.text:
$TSLA (3/16) $391.20 (-0.96%)

Tesla broke below its 200-day moving average for the first time since September. Down over 20% from ATH, officially in bear market territory. Institutions are trimming positions.

But China sales surged 35% in Jan-Feb to 127,728 units, outpacing BYD. Europe registrations also +10% YoY. Sales recovery signals are emerging.

P/E 362x, target $407 (+4%). Below both 50-day ($420) and 200-day ($425). This Saturday's Terafab AI chip launch is the only card left for a near-term reversal.

witty.hook: Tesla enters bear market, but is launching a chip factory on Saturday
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/16) Below the 200-day MA, -20% from ATH. Bear market official. Meanwhile, Musk is opening an AI chip factory on Saturday. Peak dramatic timing.

1/4
thread:
Institutions flee (Calydon -62%) while China sales surge +35%. BYD falling, Tesla rising. Good news and bad news at the same time.

2/4

A P/E 362x stock in bear territory building its own AI chip fab. If it works, genius. If not, well, there's always next quarter.

3/4

$391. Target $407 (+4%). Saturday Terafab works: game changer. Doesn't: $350 might be next.

4/4

witty.hook: Tesla enters bear market, but is launching a chip factory on Saturday
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/16) $391.20 (-0.96%)

Below the 200-day, -20% from ATH. Bear market official. Institutions running for the exits. Classic bearish setup.

Except China sales are up 35%, beating BYD. And there's an AI chip factory launching Saturday. Only Tesla would announce a moonshot project right as it enters bear territory.

Target $407 (+4%). If Terafab works, bear market exit rocket. If not, $350 starts looking realistic.

empathy.hook: Tesla holders, 200-day MA break hurts but Saturday is coming
empathy.text:
$TSLA (3/16) $391.20 (-0.96%)

Below the 200-day MA for the first time since September. If you're holding Tesla, this has been tough. Institutions pulling out doesn't help either.

But China sales at 127,728 units (+35% YoY) is genuinely encouraging. BYD dropped while Tesla gained. Europe also growing for the first time in a year.

Saturday's Terafab launch is confirmed. This could be the turning point. Target $407 (+4%). Give it until Saturday before making any decisions.
